notes: Three visitors at the Static Drive-In: the projectionist (in the booth, on the board at start, talks through the intercom), a family in a truck (arrives at the counter at turn 2), the kid alone in row three (arrives at the counter at turn 4).

visitors.name: Theo (the projectionist)
visitors.lore_text: Theo Bourgeois. Projectionist at the Static Drive-In since 2002. Knows Natalie's documentary work. Has been worried about her since the June episode. Keeps the intercom on a hair-trigger because of it.
visitors.tutorial_note: Theo connects through RING BELL — your ring tells him you're present at the counter. The intercom click after is the exchange.

steps.greet: He keys the intercom at 10:51: 'Nine minutes, Nat.' He always does the time check. The hiss after he releases the key is a comfort.
steps.listen: At 10:56 he keys again: 'Nat, the static's doing the thing.' He has been watching it from the booth. He doesn't, this evening, say more than that.
steps.deliver: After you ring the bell he keys: 'Nice, Nat.' He cues feature two. The lot's headlights come on in waves.
steps.sit_with: After feature two starts he comes down from the booth at 11:18 with two cups of cold coffee. He stands at the counter with you for fifteen minutes. He does not, in those fifteen minutes, say anything. The presence is the sit-with.

visitors.name: The Hebert family
visitors.lore_text: Marie Hebert, Antoine Hebert, and their daughter Lou (seven). Drove in from Madisonville for the Wednesday double-feature. Marie does the ordering. Antoine pretends to read the candy case labels. Lou is at her mother's hip.
pre_arrival_hints: A pickup door slams in row two.
Three sets of footsteps on the gravel toward the snack bar porch.
The door bell jingles. Marie at the counter, Antoine reading the candy case, Lou at her mother's hip.
visitors.tutorial_note: The Heberts connect through POUR SODA (you pour two large) plus a Reese's from the case. Don't undercount the popcorn — large means large.

steps.greet: Marie says: 'Hi Natalie.' She knows your name from the documentary's first episode, which ran on the local Sunday-afternoon show. She doesn't, ever, mention it. You appreciate this.
steps.listen: Antoine tells you, while you pour the sodas, that Lou learned to whistle today. Lou demonstrates. The whistle is a real whistle. Marie laughs the laugh that means she's been the one who taught Lou.
steps.deliver: You hand off: popcorn large with butter, two large sodas, Reese's, four napkins. You hand Lou her napkin separately. Lou nods like an adult.
steps.sit_with: Before they go back to the truck Marie sets a five-dollar bill on the counter in front of you and says: 'Tip jar.' She walks out before you can say anything. The five sits on the counter for the rest of the intermission.

visitors.name: The kid in row three (Eli)
visitors.lore_text: Eli Comeaux, nine. His mother brought him to the drive-in and fell asleep before feature one. He came to the counter alone with a folded five-dollar bill. He has been watching the static the whole intermission.
visitors.placeholder_name: a small figure at the counter
pre_arrival_hints: A small bicycle is leaning against the snack bar porch.
The kid is at the counter alone.
He's holding a folded-up five. He's looking at the candy case.
visitors.tutorial_note: Eli connects through pop_corn + a soda, and through you not asking about his mother. Don't ask. Hand him the food. He'll come back.

steps.greet: You ask him what he'd like. He says: 'Popcorn, please. And a small soda. Please.'
steps.listen: He asks you, while you pour the soda, if the static on the screen is a real thing. You ask him what he means. He says: 'I see it doing letters.' He says it the way kids say things that are real. You think about your June episode for one second and decide not to.
steps.deliver: You hand him the popcorn and the soda. You tell him, gently: 'It's just the projector cycling. It's nothing.' He nods. He hands you the five. You give him a dollar fifty back. He takes the change and the food without spilling.
steps.sit_with: Before he leaves he says: 'Are you okay, lady.' You say: 'I'm okay.' He says: 'You should sleep more.' He turns and goes back to his mother's truck. He gets in carefully so as not to wake her. The exchange is the exchange.

passive_witnesses.name: The drive-in screen
passive_witnesses.lore_text: Single screen, the lot side up. Static between features. The static, by 11:00 PM, gives way to the projector's first cue.
